title: France's traditional parties dream of a return to the classic left-right contest
subhead: The Socialists and right-wing Les Républicains can boast of successes in the second round of France's municipal elections on March 22. But both parties remain deeply weakened by the lack of a clear direction and the fragmentation of the political landscape, writes Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

subhead: The nationalist prime minister is taking advantage of opportunities to rally his foreign supporters, particularly Europeans, in Budapest ahead of the April 12 legislative elections, which could prove difficult for him.

title: Mette Frederiksen heads into Danish elections as a global figure, but a divisive one domestically
subhead: The leader of the Social Democrats, in power since 2019, seeks reelection on Tuesday. While her support for Ukraine and her firm stance against Donald Trump regarding Greenland have earned her recognition on the European stage, her party has been struggling in the polls.

title: US Senate confirms Markwayne Mullin as new chief of Department of Homeland Security
subhead: Mullin, a Trump ally, replaces Kristi Noem, who was fired earlier this month for her role in the large-scale operation against undocumented migrants in Minnesota.

title: Five affordable vegetarian restaurants in the Paris region
subhead: Craving a quinoa arancini, an Asian-inspired salad, or a coconut dessert? Here is our selection of places in Paris and the suburbs where you can enjoy quality vegetarian food for under €20.

title: The UAE reorganizes its arms supply network for Sudanese paramilitaries
subhead: Abu Dhabi is seeking to develop new logistical routes from Sudan's neighboring countries, such as Ethiopia and the Central African Republic, to support the Rapid Support Forces.

title: Vladimir Putin, the conductor of a 'special musical operation' in Europe
subhead: Barred from the West, Valery Gergiev, maestro at the helm of Russia's two leading opera houses and cultural ambassador for Vladimir Putin, is seeking a return to conducting in Europe's major concert halls. In this cultural arena, Moscow is shifting from 'soft power' to 'sharp power.'
